folio.label: 171v
folio.l: a gret vertu of ¹feccion ,

but euäy man may nat haue , the ¹fecci-
on æ ©e seken ,

ne I am nat of the noumbre , of the rigút ¹fit
men ,

for myn herte may neuäe be in pees , vn to the tyme , it
be venged ,

And al be it so , æ it was gret ¹ile to myne enemys ,
to do me a vileynye , in takynge vengeau~ce vp oon me ,

©et to-
ken æei noon hede of the ¹ile , but fulfilleden here wykked wyl
& here corage

¢ And æäfore me thynketú , men oughten nat
repreue me , thougú I putte me , in a litel ¹ile , for to venge
me ,

And thougú I do a gret excesse , æ is to seyn ; æ I venge
oon outrage by another

¢ A quod Dame Prudence , ©e seyn
©oure wil , and as ©ow liketú ,

But in no cas of the world , a man
shulde nat don outge ne excesse , for to vengen hi~

¢ For Cassidore
seitú ; As yuele dotú he , æ vengetú hi~ by outge , as he æ dotú æe
outge ,

And æäfore ©e shuln venge ©ow , after the ordre of rigút , æ
is to seyn ; by the lawe , & nat biexcesse ne by outge

¢ And also
if ©e wol venge ©ow of the outge of ©oure aduäsaries , in other
manäe than rigút comaundetú , ©e synnen ,

¢ And æäfore seitú
Senek ; æ a man shal neuäe venge shrewednesse by shrewednesse ,

And if ©e seye , æ rigút axetú , a man to defende violence , by vio-
lence , & figútyngö by figútyngö

¢ Certes ©e seye sotú , whan
the defence is doon a noon , w outen int äualle , or witú outen
tariynge , or delay
for to defende hi~ , And nat for to venge ,
And it bihouetú , æ a man putte swich atattem¹au~ce in his defence ,

æ men haue no cause ne matere , to repäue hi~ æ defendetú hi~
of excesse & outge , for elles were it a geyn resou~

¢ Parde ©e
knowen wel , æ ©e maken no deffence as now , for to deffende
©ow , But for to venge ©ow ,

And so sewitú it , æ ©e han no wyl
to do ©oure dede attemprely ,

And æäfore me thynketú , æ pacien-
ce is good ¢ For Salamon seitú ; æ he that is nat pacient
shal haue gret harm

¢ Certes quod Melibe , I gunte ©ow , æ
whan a man is inpacient & wrootú , of æ that touchetú hi~ nat ,
& that a¹tenetú nat vn to hi~ , thougú it harme hi~ , it is no
wonder ;

For the lawe seitú ; æ he is coupable , æ entremetetú
or medletú w swicú thyngö , as a¹tenetú nat vn to hi~

¢ And
Salamon seitú ; æ he that entremetetú of noise , or strif , of a
nother man , is like to hi~ , æ taketú an hound by the erys ,

For
rigút as he æ taketú a straunge hound by the erys , is other whi-
le biten , w the hound ,

rigút in the same wyse is it resoun , æ
he haue harm , æ by his impacience , medletú hi~ of the noise
of a nother man , where as it a¹tenetú nat vn to hi~ ,

But ©e
knowe wel æ this dede , æ is to seyn ; my grief & my dissese
touchetú me rigút ny

¢ And æäfore æougú I be wrotú and inpa-
cient , it is no mäueille ,

& sauynge ©oure gce , I kan nat se
æ it mygút gretly harm me , thougú I took vengeaunce ,,
For
